description_long: This is an ornately decorated ceramic vase with a tall, narrow neck and a single, high-arching handle that extends from the body to the rim. The body of the vase is adorned with intricate painted motifs, including floral patterns and classical figures, rendered in earthy tones of black, terracotta, and cream. The neck features vertical striping and a band of geometric designs, while the shoulder is encircled by a garland of stylized flowers. The handle and spout are sculpted, with the handle terminating in a decorative flourish and the spout shaped like an animal's head. The base is round and flat, providing stability for upright display. The overall craftsmanship and detailed ornamentation suggest it is inspired by ancient Greek or Etruscan pottery, suitable for display in a refined urban setting.
description: A tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a high-arching handle, ornate painted motifs, and a sculpted animal-head spout, finished in earthy black, terracotta, and cream tones.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main painted scene with classical figures and floral motifs, as well as the sculpted animal-head spout and the start of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full curve of the handle, the animal-head spout, and the continuation of the floral and geometric patterns.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the handle and a large, symmetrical floral motif, with less emphasis on figurative decoration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals more of the handle's attachment and additional floral and geometric designs, with a partial view of the main painted scene.
